evals.name: real-init-happy-path
evals.prompt: Initialize this project for me — make sure every tool the skills in .codex/skills need is actually installed on this machine.
evals.expected_output: Runs the dependency checker against .codex/skills, reports that python3, node, and reportlab are all present, identifies the Codex @chrome plugin as a browser dependency, checks whether the Chrome plugin capability is available in the current Codex session, installs nothing (nothing is missing), and gives an honest summary: connected if the Chrome capability is present, otherwise ready except for the browser connection.
assertions: Runs check_skill_deps.py (or otherwise inspects .codex/skills) rather than guessing dependencies
Identifies the Codex @chrome plugin as a browser dependency
Checks current Codex tool/skill availability for the Chrome plugin capability instead of relying only on the checker's installed:null field
Does NOT run any pip/npm/brew install command (everything CLI-installable is already present)
Final summary is honest: reports @chrome connected when Chrome capability is present, otherwise says the project is ready except for the browser connection

evals.name: fresh-clone-full-policy
evals.prompt: I just cloned a repo whose skills live in .codex/skills/ww-setup/evals/fixtures/fresh-skills. Set it up so those skills will run — install whatever they need.
evals.expected_output: Runs the checker against the fixture root, then applies the install policy: auto-installs the missing pip package (pyfiglet) WITHOUT asking, does NOT silently run `npm install -g cowsay` (flags it for confirmation since it's a heavy/global install), and checks current Codex Chrome capability before deciding whether the Codex @chrome plugin is connected or still needs user setup. Final report distinguishes the outcomes.
assertions: Runs check_skill_deps.py with --root pointing at the fixture folder (fresh-skills)
Auto-installs the missing pip package pyfiglet via pip without first asking the user for permission
Does NOT run `npm install -g cowsay`; instead surfaces it as needing user confirmation before a heavy/global install
Checks current Codex tool/skill availability for the Chrome plugin capability and does not attempt to install @chrome from the CLI
Final summary clearly separates what was auto-installed, what needs confirmation, and whether @chrome is connected or still needs user setup

evals.name: will-scraper-run-honest-report
evals.prompt: Will the WaterlooWorks scraper actually run on this laptop, or am I missing something?
evals.expected_output: Checks the dependencies for ww-scrape-jobs, confirms the CLI tools it needs (node, python3) are present, clearly surfaces that it additionally requires the Codex @chrome plugin, and checks current Codex Chrome capability before answering whether the scraper can actually run.
assertions: Inspects the actual dependencies of the scraper (via the checker) instead of guessing
Confirms node and python3 are installed
Surfaces that the scraper requires the Codex @chrome plugin
Does NOT give an unqualified 'yes it will run' unless current Codex Chrome capability is available
